entities.description: The complete parchment codex used by Davies for pages 1-110. Parts 1-2 contain the English Brut through Edward III. Part 3, written by two secretary hands in or soon after 1461, contains the continuation printed by Davies; early leaves of that continuation survive only as damaged fragments.

holding.notes: Acquired by the Bodleian through James P. R. Lyell's bequest. De la Mare's official catalog is the exact reviewed item description.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Lyell 34

physicalDescription.extent: vii + 219 leaves (the catalog notes that the physical sequence is effectively 227 leaves because of inserted fragment leaves 144a-h)
notes: Target portion: part 3, fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v; fols. 215-219v are blank.
The codex's earlier Brut text is not part of Davies's printed pages 1-110 but should be included if a complete-manuscript facsimile can be obtained.
The continuation has lost or badly damaged leaves near its beginning.

entities.description: A fifteenth-century English Brut manuscript and the base witness for William Marx's modern edition. Its continuation to 1450 includes, for 1415-1419, the same unusual text as the Davies Chronicle; a missing quire and a half probably once continued the text to 1461. It is a parallel witness, not a source used by Davies.

holding.notes: The catalog marks the original restricted and directs researchers to surrogate copies.

William Marx's An English Chronicle 1377-1461: A New Edition (2003)
Albinia de la Mare's Catalogue of the Collection of Medieval Manuscripts Bequeathed to the Bodleian Library, Oxford, by James P. R. Lyell (1971)

object.description: Richard Fox's unique manuscript witness and chronicle

relations.subjectLabel: Anna Probert, Richard Fox of St Albans: Compiler of a Chronicle of English History (2023)

reviewNotes: The cataloged object is Davies's edited compilation, not a single medieval work: it joins the anonymous Davies Chronicle, selected Richard Fox passages, Latin Eulogium supplements, Davies's notes, and an index.

Davies treated the main chronicle as one compiler's work. Modern scholarship distinguishes two continuations, covering 1377-1437 and 1440-1461; anonymous responsibility is therefore retained without inventing named authors.
NLW MS 21608D is a parallel and now-preferred witness for the modern Marx edition. It was not the source of Davies's text or of the U-M encoding.

The surviving Lyell codex contains the earlier Brut before the portion Davies printed. Any reproduction request should ask for the complete manuscript, then associate this work with fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v.

QUB's MS 59 finding aid identifies a microfilm of Lyell 34 as MS 59/160b, but the public description says individual reels may reproduce manuscripts in whole or in part; the reel's coverage remains unverified.
Negative digitization findings are date-bounded to 11 July 2026 and should not be treated as permanent statements about repository services.

lineage.summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of University of Michigan CME item ACV5981.0001.001, a 2006 Level-1 TEI encoding made by uncorrected OCR from John Silvester Davies's 1856 Camden Society edition. The searchable XML transcribes printed pages 1-118 and retains page-break links through page 228, but pages 119-228 have no OCR text. Davies printed the main anonymous Middle English prose chronicle on pages 1-110 from the manuscript now Bodleian Library MS. Lyell 34, part 3 (fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v); selected Richard Fox material from Woburn Abbey MS 181 on pages 111-118; and Latin supplements from the continuation of the Eulogium in British Library Cotton MS Galba E VII on pages 119-142. A later edition by William Marx uses National Library of Wales MS 21608D as its base and Lyell 34 for comparison, so it is a related modern edition rather than an ancestor of the CME text. A complete public scan of the 1856 print edition is cached. No complete public digital facsimile was found for any manuscript witness; the manifest records whole-codex request routes and maps the target portion whenever the catalog evidence permits.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous compilers; Richard Fox and an anonymous Eulogium compiler for appended selections
catalogSummary.date: Main continuation compiled in stages and completed after 4 March 1461, probably before 22 May 1471; Fox source completed 1448; first published 1856
catalogSummary.region: England; St Albans for the Richard Fox component; main continuation's exact place unresolved
catalogSummary.title: An English Chronicle of the Reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI

catalogingSubject.label: An English Chronicle of the Reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The edited compilation published by John Silvester Davies in 1856 and represented by CME item ACV5981.0001.001. It contains the anonymous Middle English prose continuation commonly called the Davies Chronicle (printed pp. 1-110), selected passages from Richard Fox's chronicle (pp. 111-118), Latin supplements from the continuation of the Eulogium Historiarum (pp. 119-142), Davies's notes (pp. 143-212), and an index (pp. 213-228). This record does not collapse those components into one medieval work and does not treat the U-M encoding, the repository XML, any manuscript codex, or William Marx's later edition as the same intellectual object.

contentParts.label: Anonymous English Chronicle, 1377-1461

locators.value: reign divisions through the end of Henry VI

notes: Davies printed all of Lyell 34 part 3. The earlier Brut in the same complete codex is outside this printed part.
contentParts.summary: Two anonymous Middle English prose continuations of the Brut tradition, arranged by Davies under the reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI and ending with Edward IV's accession.

contentParts.label: Appendix from Richard Fox's Chronicle
contentParts.summary: Selected accounts of the eighteenth and nineteenth years of Richard II and the Parliament at Bury and death of Humphrey, duke of Gloucester, supplied from Woburn Abbey MS 181.

contentParts.label: Supplementary Additions from the Continuation of the Eulogium

locators.value: selected passages in the Eulogium at fols. 18r-208v; individual folios printed with each supplement

notes: These pages are not searchable in the CME XML.
contentParts.summary: Latin passages printed by Davies from Cotton MS Galba E VII to document sources, supply context, and supplement damaged or missing material in the main manuscript.

contentParts.label: Notes
notes: Represented by PB milestones but not searchable text in the CME XML.
contentParts.summary: Davies's historical and textual notes to the medieval texts.

contentParts.label: Index
notes: Represented by PB milestones but not searchable text in the CME XML.

dateStatements.display: Compiled in stages; final continuation after Edward IV's accession on 4 March 1461 and probably before Henry VI's death on 22 May 1471
notes: Davies inferred the terminal bounds from the narrative. Modern scholarship distinguishes an earlier continuation through 1437 and a later continuation covering 1440-1461, so this range describes the final compilation layer rather than every passage.

dateStatements.display: Richard Fox completed the main body of his chronicle in 1448; later additions include the 1447 Bury material printed by Davies
notes: The range accommodates the relationship between Fox's 1448 completion statement and later additions; it is not a date for Davies's selection.

dateStatements.display: Bodleian MS. Lyell 34, part 3, written in or soon after 1461
notes: This is a witness-production date. It does not prove that both continuation layers were composed at the same moment or by the manuscript's two scribes.

dateStatements.display: First published as Davies's Camden Society edition in 1856

extent.description: The 1856 edition has xvi preliminary pages and 228 numbered pages. Repository inspection finds 244 PB, 439 P, 13 LG, and 96 L elements. The searchable XML covers printed pp. 1-118; later pages survive as page milestones without transcribed text. Five top-level catalog parts model the main chronicle, Fox appendix, Eulogium supplements, notes, and index.

formStatements.description: A predominantly prose chronicle compilation with prose scholarly apparatus; the CME XML marks 96 embedded verse lines in 13 line groups, which do not make the whole work mixed-form.
The encoding contains 13 LG elements and 96 L elements, embedded within a much larger prose structure.

scope.description: Short embedded verse and document-like passages within the encoded prose

genres.label: Scholarly edition
notes: This is an edition-level genre. It does not describe the medieval source works as nineteenth-century scholarship.
The overall prose classification follows the dominant textual organization; 96 embedded verse lines in 13 groups do not justify a whole-work mixed label.
The preferred title describes Davies's publication unit. 'Davies Chronicle' and 'An English Chronicle 1377-1461' apply only to the anonymous main continuation.
Modern normalized tags are discovery aids, not assertions that medieval compilers used those categories.
The complete 1856 print facsimile is locally cached outside Git. No manuscript facsimile is locally cached because no verified complete public manuscript surrogate was found.

next_steps: Consult Marx's introduction and subsequent textual scholarship.

notes: Davies described a single compiler; modern scholarship's two-continuator model is used here without claiming two named people or an exact hand-to-author mapping.

summaries.value: Davies's 1856 volume centers on an anonymous Middle English prose continuation of the Brut tradition narrating English history from Richard II through Edward IV's accession. It adds selected Richard Fox accounts and Latin Eulogium passages to supplement the damaged principal witness, followed by extensive editorial notes and an index. The chronicle is especially concerned with deposition and succession, royal and magnate politics, parliament, warfare in France, and the crises of Lancastrian rule.

Printed pp. 1-110: the anonymous Davies Chronicle from Lyell 34. Pp. 111-118: three Richard Fox extracts from Woburn MS 181. Pp. 119-142: Latin Eulogium supplements from Cotton Galba E VII. Pp. 143-212: Davies's notes. Pp. 213-228: index. The CME searchable XML transcribes only pp. 1-118, although page milestones continue through p. 228.

The date, place, language, and responsibility statements are part-scoped because this is an edited compilation of distinct medieval sources. The Lyell and NLW manuscript dates and dialect evidence describe physical witnesses and are not automatically transferred to the abstract continuation texts.

notes: The preferred title is the main title of Davies's edited compilation; its title-page subtitle describes the appendix and Eulogium supplements separately.
titles.value: An English Chronicle of the Reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI

An English Chronicle of the Reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI, Written Before the Year 1471; with an Appendix, Containing the 18th and 19th Years of Richard II, and the Parliament at Bury St. Edmund's, 25th Henry VI; and Supplementary Additions from the Cotton MS. Chronicle Called ‘Eulogium’

notes: This conventional scholarly label must not be extended to the Richard Fox and Eulogium appendices or to Davies's apparatus.

scope.description: The main anonymous Middle English continuation only
Modern title for the two anonymous continuations
